newsSinceJun6: No material new fundamentals since June 6. The drivers of the recent move all predate the window: June 2 William Blair Growth Stock Conference presentation and an updated June 2026 investor deck (drove a ~21% pop on June 2), the April record $41M hyperscale AI customer package-level burn-in order (deliveries begin FY2027, starting late June 2026), and >$92M second-half bookings. Today's -4.5% to $91.28 appears to be consolidation/profit-taking after the parabolic run, not news. Crowding markers persist: stock trades ~40% above the ~$64 sell-side average target and heavy retail-trading-site coverage after +312% YTD.

sources.label: QuiverQuant - AEHR jumps on updated AI burn-in narrative ahead of conference
Aehr IR - William Blair 46th Annual Growth Stock Conference, June 2
TIKR - AEHR stock jumps after major AI customer production order ($41M)
StocksGuide - AEHR analyst price targets (avg ~$63.67, ~46% below price)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already moved it down for discovery after the $41M AI order and +312% YTD; nothing genuinely new since June 6 and today's -4.5% drift does not change the setup. Latency premise is largely spent at rank 94, so hold with slight negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Record $41M hyperscale AI burn-in order plus >$92M H2 bookings prove the AI processor reliability-screening thesis is real, with FY2027 deliveries starting late June.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis is now well telegraphed; at $2.9B cap and rank 94 the residual hidden-node value is small either way, and the stock is digesting the June 2 conference pop.
verdicts.bear: Price sits ~40% above the sell-side average target after +312% YTD with heavy retail coverage; customer concentration on one hyperscaler makes any order air-pocket brutal.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Fiscal Q4 results and FY2027 guidance in July; follow-on AI customer or order announcements.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order timing slip or single-customer concentration disappointment triggering a sharp de-rating from elevated levels.

verdicts.rankRationale: Already at rank 100 after yesterday's downgrade; no new WFE/HBM-relevant evidence since June 6, and the modest -4.5% pullback is consolidation, not a setup change.
verdicts.bull: Package-level burn-in for custom AI ASICs gives Aehr a genuine, growing seat at the AI test-intensity table that this page's thesis was waiting for.
verdicts.neutral: Burn-in remains peripheral to the page's core fab/HBM capacity bottleneck lens; rank 100 already reflects that indirectness.
verdicts.bear: After a 4x YTD move the name carries cycle and valuation risk far in excess of its bottleneck centrality; export controls and customer concentration add tail risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY2027 delivery ramp of the $41M hyperscale order and incremental AI bookings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation compression if AI burn-in adoption proves narrower than the conference-deck narrative.

verdicts.rankRationale: On this crowding-sensitive alpha lens, rank 43 / score 69.7 is too generous for a name trading ~40% above consensus targets with saturating retail coverage after +312% YTD and no new fundamental evidence since June 6; the 'small-cap test optionality' premise is no longer hidden or under-owned.
verdicts.bull: If silicon photonics and AI power devices require wafer-level reliability screening at scale, Aehr's installed-base head start and >$92M H2 bookings give real operating leverage.
verdicts.neutral: The optionality is now consensus; risk/reward at $91 is balanced between FY2027 ramp delivery and a crowded-trade unwind, and today's -4.5% dip barely dents the run.
verdicts.bear: Very high volatility, single-customer concentration, and a price that has already discounted multiple years of perfect execution leave negative asymmetry on any bookings pause.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New silicon photonics or second AI customer design win that would re-arm the optionality case.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating chase unwind: crowded retail positioning meeting a soft fiscal Q4 print or guidance.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already priced the discovery; no new scaling-relevant evidence since June 6 and the -4.5% day is routine digestion of the June 2 conference pop, so hold at rank 47.
verdicts.bull: Burn-in intensity per AI processor is rising, and the $41M hyperscale order plus >$92M H2 bookings show Aehr converting that scaling tailwind into revenue.
verdicts.neutral: A small, concentrated test niche within the scaling stack; score 18.9 at rank 47 already reflects limited bottleneck weight.
verdicts.bear: At ~$2.9B and 4x YTD, the stock embeds a scaling narrative its single-hyperscaler order book does not yet support; analyst targets sit far below the price.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY2027 guidance in July quantifying the AI burn-in ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical SiC/EV softness plus AI order lumpiness producing a guidance miss into a crowded register.
